revision.comment: 秋田県にかほ市の神社
改文を含めてやや加筆（範疇追加などなど）
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像= |所在地=秋田県にかほ市象潟町小滝字奈曽沢1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000}} |祭神=[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 |社格= |創建=[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭=6月第2土曜日 |神事 = 小滝のチョウクライロ舞 }}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]][[象潟町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8[[柱 (曖昧さ回避)|柱]] ** [[神仏分離]]以前は[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]が[[祭神]]であった。
== 由緒 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name=akitajiten>『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁|慈覚大師円仁]]が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm 東北文庫 高橋富雄「東北の慈覚大師伝説霊場」] * [http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp 国指定文化財等データベース] * [http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm 金峯神社]（鳥海山小滝舞楽保存会）
{{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:きんほうしんしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:修験道]] [[Category:にかほ市]]

{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像= |所在地=秋田県にかほ市象潟町小滝字奈曽沢1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000}} |祭神=[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 |社格= |創建=[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭=6月第2土曜日 |神事 = 小滝のチョウクライロ舞 }}

== 歴史 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name=akitajiten>『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定有形文化財 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm 東北文庫 高橋富雄「東北の慈覚大師伝説霊場」] * [http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp 国指定文化財等データベース] * [http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm 金峯神社]（鳥海山小滝舞楽保存会）
{{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:きんほうしんしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:修験道]] [[Category:にかほ市]]

{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=にかほ市}} |所在地=[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝字奈曽沢1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000}} |祭神=[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 |社格= |創建=[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭=6月第2土曜日 |神事 = 小滝のチョウクライロ舞 }}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 ** [[神仏分離]]以前は[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]が[[祭神]]であった。
== 歴史 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name=akitajiten>『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定有形文化財 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm 東北文庫 高橋富雄「東北の慈覚大師伝説霊場」] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース] * [http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm 金峯神社]（鳥海山小滝舞楽保存会）

'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 ** [[神仏分離]]以前は[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]が[[祭神]]であった。
== 歴史 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定有形文化財 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車

{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像=[[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|金峰神社]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝字奈曽沢1 |位置={{coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金峰神社}} |祭神=[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 |社格= |創建=[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭=6月第2土曜日 |神事 = 小滝のチョウクライロ舞 |地図={{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=金峰神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 ** [[神仏分離]]以前は[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]が[[祭神]]であった。
== 歴史 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定有形文化財 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm 東北文庫 高橋富雄「東北の慈覚大師伝説霊場」] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm |title=金峯神社 |date=20070825032732}}（鳥海山小滝舞楽保存会）

'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝にある[[神社]]である。

{{Shrine | Name = Kinpō Shrine | Image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)
{{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Shrine | Name = Kinpō Shrine | Image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)
{{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Shrine | Name = Kinpō Shrine | Image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)
{{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)
{{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)
{{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}}

The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

{{Shrine | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|Kinpō Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinpō Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2年}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16年}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nikaho City|ja|にかほ市}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Jōwa 3|ja|斉衡}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Ryūzan-ji|ja|龍山寺}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|Meiji 2|ja|明治2nd year }} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|2004|ja|平成16th year }}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|ja|象潟駅}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像=[[File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg|260px|金峰神社]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝字奈曽沢1 |位置={{coord|39|10|55|N|139|56|32|E|scale:10000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金峰神社}} |祭神=[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 |社格= |創建=[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭=6月第2土曜日 |神事 = 小滝のチョウクライロ舞 |地図={{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=金峰神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぽうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[にかほ市]]象潟町小滝にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]]ほか8柱 ** [[神仏分離]]以前は[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]が[[祭神]]であった。
== 歴史 == 社伝では[[役小角|役行者]]が小滝の少彦名神に[[蔵王権現]]を合わせ祀り<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』[[秋田魁新報]]編、1981年 ISBN 4870200074</ref>、[[斉衡]]3年（[[856年]]）、[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が[[鳥海山]]の巨人「手長足長」を退治した際に[[大物忌神|鳥海大権現]]と[[蔵王権現]]を奉じたとされている。[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の[[別当寺]]であったともいわれるが、[[江戸時代]]には「蔵王堂」と呼ばれ、龍山寺を[[別当]]とする[[宮寺|宮寺一体]]の形態で[[鳥海修験]]の中心地の一つとして信仰を集めた。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に「鳥海神社」と改名し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）には境内社の熊野神明社を[[合祀]]して現社名「金峰神社」に改称した。
== 神事 == 例祭で演じられる「チョウクライロ舞」は、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）2月、「[[小滝のチョウクライロ舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 文化財 == === 秋田県指定有形文化財 === *木造[[狛犬]]1対（彫刻。昭和45年4月2日指定） *木造観音菩薩立像1躯（彫刻。昭和46年1月9日指定） - [[平安時代]]末期の作ともされる[[杉]]の[[一木造]] *木造蔵王権現立像3躯（彫刻。同上）
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[象潟駅]]から横岡行バスで15分、奈曽滝下車
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm 東北文庫 高橋富雄「東北の慈覚大師伝説霊場」] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm |title=金峯神社 |date=20070825032732}}（鳥海山小滝舞楽保存会）
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんほうしんしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:修験道]] [[Category:鳥海山]] [[Category:にかほ市の歴史]] [[Category:にかほ市の建築物]] [[Category:秋田県指定有形文化財]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|qid=Q847550}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|WD=Q847550}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
