<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647033]] Wikidata name: Kinpou-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|WD=Q847550}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nikaho, Akita|Nikaho City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]], along with eight other deities. ** Before the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In [[:en:Saikō|Jōwa 3]] (856), [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]].
During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the [[:en:Longshan Temple|Ryūzan-ji]] temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in [[:en:2004|Heisei 16]].
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bosatsu]] (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]], alight at [[:en:Kisakata Station|Kisakata Station]], take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|WD=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|WD=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|WD=Q847550}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|WD=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|WD=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|WD=Q847550|1=Nikaho, Akita}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|WD=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|1=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|WD=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji|1=Longshan Temple}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=Bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16|1=2004}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16|1=2004}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|WD=Q847550|1=Nikaho, Akita}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|WD=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|WD=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|WD=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|1=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|WD=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji|1=Longshan Temple}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=Bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16|1=2004}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|WD=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.

The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|qid=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|1=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|qid=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji|1=Longshan Temple}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=Bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16|1=2004}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|qid=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|qid=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.touhoku.com/00x-10-26tt-mitinoku12.htm Tōhoku Bunko – Takahashi Tomio, "Legendary Sites of Jikaku Daishi in Tōhoku"] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Property Database] * {{Wayback|url=http://1st.geocities.jp/yuinajittan/index.htm|title=Kinpō Shrine|date=20070825032732}} (Chōkai Kotaki Bugaku Preservation Society)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinpou-jinja (Nikaho)]][[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (にかほ市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinpō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kinpou Shrine, Nikaho.jpg | location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|qid=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, Kisakata Town, Kotaki Aza Nasozawa 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|39.181944|N|139.942222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} and eight other deities | Rank = | Established = {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|qid=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856) | Main Hall = | Other names = | festival = Second Saturday in June | Rituals = Kotaki's Chōkura-iro Dance | Map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=10|lat_sec=55|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=32|label=Kinpō Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nikaho|qid=Q847550|1=Nikaho, Akita}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }}
'''Kinpō Shrine''' (金峰神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nikaho, Akita|en|Nikaho, Akita|qid=Q847550|lt=Nikaho City|1=Nikaho, Akita}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Deities == * The main deity is {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}, along with eight other deities. ** Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Chōkai Daigongen) and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} were also enshrined.
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|qid=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}} as a site to enshrine both Sukunabikona and {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}.<ref name="akitajiten">『秋田大百科事典』秋田魁新報編、1981st year ISBN 4870200074</ref> In {{ill|Saikō|en|Saikō|qid=Q1324664|lt=Jōwa 3|1=Saikō}} (856), {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|qid=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) subdued the giant "Tenaga Ashinaga" of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|qid=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} and enshrined the deities Chōkai Daigongen and Zaō Gongen. The shrine may have served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} for {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|en|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|qid=Q11173679|lt=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|1=Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine}}.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as "Zaō-dō" and associated with the {{ill|Longshan Temple|en|Longshan Temple|qid=Q1338322|lt=Ryūzan-ji|1=Longshan Temple}} temple. It formed a {{ill|Miyadera|ja|宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyadera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=Bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396755}} system and was a central hub of {{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}}, attracting many devotees.
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (1869), the shrine was renamed "Chōkai Shrine." By {{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}} (1913), it was further renamed "Kinpō Shrine," incorporating the Kumano Shinmei Shrine on its grounds.
== Rituals == The shrine's annual festival features the "Chōkura-iro Dance," which was designated a National {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} in {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=Heisei 16|1=2004}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Akita Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * A pair of wooden {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} (designated April 2, 1970) * A standing wooden statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bosatsu|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (designated January 9, 1971) – A single-piece carving of {{ill|Japanese cedar|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese cedar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q147388}}, possibly from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. * Three standing wooden statues of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} (designated January 9, 1971)
== Transportation == * By Rail: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|qid=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}, alight at {{ill|Kisakata Station|en|Kisakata Station|qid=Q6416385|lt=Kisakata Station|1=Kisakata Station}}, take a Yokoka-bound bus for 15 minutes, and disembark at Naso Falls.
== References == {{Reflist}}
